title: rhodanthemum atlanticum subsp. atlanticum
intro: Rhodanthemum atlanticum subsp. atlanticum, also known as the Atlantic orazul, is a flowering plant native to the western Mediterranean region, characterized by its blue-purple flowers and gray-green leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Consuming Rhodanthemum atlanticum subsp. atlanticum has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
